data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on device R2 is down, preventing route exchange with its neighbor(s). Desired outcome: restore the OSPF adjacency and resume normal route propagation.

data.alert_description: OSPF adjacency on device R2 is down, preventing route exchange with its neighbor(s). Desired outcome: restore the OSPF adjacency and resume normal route propagation.

evidence.detail: router1: Serial0/1/1 sees neighbor 3.3.3.3 at 10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full cost 50
router3: Serial0/1/0 sees neighbor 1.1.1.1 at 10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full cost 49
router2: Serial0/1/1 sees neighbor 3.3.3.3 at 10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full cost 49
router3: Serial0/1/1 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full cost 50
router1: Serial0/1/0 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init cost 50
router2: Serial0/1/0 present in OSPF but no neighbor reported in this snapshot

recent_changes.summary: Observed OSPF neighbor between router1 Serial0/1/1 and router3 Serial0/1/0 (both reported adjacency)
Observed OSPF neighbor between router2 Serial0/1/1 and router3 Serial0/1/1 (both reported adjacency)
Observed OSPF neighbor (init) from router1 Serial0/1/0 toward router2 Serial0/1/0 (router2 reported interface present but no neighbor)

facts.claim: router1: OSPF process 1, router_id 1.1.1.1. Interfaces: Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init); GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1 (no neighbors)

router2: OSPF process 1, router_id 2.2.2.2. Interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1 (no neighbors); Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (no neighbors reported)

router3: OSPF process 1, router_id 3.3.3.3. Interfaces: Loopback1 state Loopback cost 1 (no neighbors); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full)

facts.claim: router1: OSPF process 1, router_id 1.1.1.1. Interfaces: Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init); GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1 (no neighbors)

router2: OSPF process 1, router_id 2.2.2.2. Interfaces: GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1 (no neighbors); Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (no neighbors reported)

router3: OSPF process 1, router_id 3.3.3.3. Interfaces: Loopback1 state Loopback cost 1 (no neighbors); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full)

root_causes.type: Unidirectional connectivity (RX loss) on R2 Serial0/1/0
root_causes.cause: R2 is not receiving or processing OSPF Hellos from R1 on the Serial0/1/0 link, so R1 progresses to 'Init' while R2 reports no neighbor—preventing adjacency completion.
evidence: router1: Serial0/1/0 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init (shows R1 has seen R2's Hellos but adjacency stuck at INIT).
router2: Serial0/1/0 present in OSPF but no neighbors reported in this snapshot (R2 reports no neighbor on that interface).
router3 adjacencies and R2<>R3 link are full, indicating R2's OSPF process is otherwise functional and the problem is localized to the R1<->R2 link.

root_causes.type: Inbound packet filtering (ACL/firewall) on R2 blocking OSPF from R1
root_causes.cause: An access-control rule or filtering on R2 could be dropping or rejecting OSPF Hello/LSA packets from R1, producing the observed asymmetry (R1 sees R2, R2 sees no neighbor).
evidence: Asymmetric neighbor visibility: router1 shows neighbor on Serial0/1/0 in INIT, while router2 shows no neighbor on the same interface—consistent with R2 not receiving/accepting OSPF packets from R1.
R2 forms full adjacency with R3 on a different serial interface, implying OSPF is enabled and working generally on R2 (points to a localized policy/filter on the R1-facing interface).

missing_info: show interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 (status, IP, input/output packet counters, CRC/errors) and same on router1 for the link
show ip ospf interface Serial0/1/0 (and running-config for that interface) on both router1 and router2 to confirm OSPF timers, authentication, and network type
show running-config / show access-lists or any interface ACLs applied inbound on router2 Serial0/1/0

data.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on device R2 is down on Serial0/1/0 (R2 not receiving OSPF Hellos from R1). Impact: routes are not exchanged with R1. Desired outcome: restore the OSPF adjacency and resume route propagation.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is not receiving OSPF Hellos from R1 (RX loss or inbound filtering), preventing adjacency formation.
plan.fix_summary: On router2 Serial0/1/0: remove any inbound ACL that could block OSPF, reset the interface to clear RX state, and ensure OSPF interface parameters are set to standard values (point-to-point network, hello/dead timers 10/40).

parsed.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on device R2 is down on Serial0/1/0 (R2 not receiving OSPF Hellos from R1). Impact: routes are not exchanged with R1. Desired outcome: restore the OSPF adjacency and resume route propagation.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is not receiving OSPF Hellos from R1 (RX loss or inbound filtering), preventing adjacency formation.
plan.fix_summary: On router2 Serial0/1/0: remove any inbound ACL that could block OSPF, reset the interface to clear RX state, and ensure OSPF interface parameters are set to standard values (point-to-point network, hello/dead timers 10/40).

data.alert_description: OSPF adjacency on device R2 is down on Serial0/1/0 (R2 not receiving OSPF Hellos from R1). Impact: routes are not exchanged with R1. Desired outcome: restore the OSPF adjacency and resume route propagation.

evidence.detail: router1: Serial0/1/1 sees neighbor 3.3.3.3 at 10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full cost 50
router3: Serial0/1/0 sees neighbor 1.1.1.1 at 10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full cost 49
router2: Serial0/1/1 sees neighbor 3.3.3.3 at 10.0.0.6 state ospf-nbr-full cost 49

facts.claim: router1: OSPF process 1, router_id 1.1.1.1. Interfaces: Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 3.3.3.3 @10.0.0.9 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init); GigabitEthernet0/0/0 state DR cost 1 (no neighbors)

router3: OSPF process 1, router_id 3.3.3.3. Interfaces: Loopback1 state Loopback cost 1 (no neighbors); Serial0/1/0 state Point-to-Point cost 49 (neighbor 1.1.1.1 @10.0.0.10 state ospf-nbr-full); Serial0/1/1 state Point-to-Point cost 50 (neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.5 state ospf-nbr-full)

root_causes.type: Unidirectional connectivity (RX loss) on R2 Serial0/1/0
root_causes.cause: R2 is not receiving or processing OSPF Hellos from R1 on the Serial0/1/0 link, so R1 progresses to 'Init' while R2 reports no neighbor—preventing adjacency completion.
evidence: router1: Serial0/1/0 sees neighbor 2.2.2.2 @10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-init (shows R1 has seen R2's Hellos but adjacency stuck at INIT).
router2: Serial0/1/0 present in OSPF but no neighbors reported in this snapshot (R2 reports no neighbor on that interface).
router3 adjacencies and R2<>R3 link are full, indicating R2's OSPF process is otherwise functional and the problem is localized to the R1<->R2 link.

root_causes.type: Inbound packet filtering (ACL/firewall) on R2 blocking OSPF from R1
root_causes.cause: An access-control rule or filtering on R2 could be dropping or rejecting OSPF Hello/LSA packets from R1, producing the observed asymmetry (R1 sees R2, R2 sees no neighbor).
evidence: Asymmetric neighbor visibility: router1 shows neighbor on Serial0/1/0 in INIT, while router2 shows no neighbor on the same interface—consistent with R2 not receiving/accepting OSPF packets from R1.
R2 forms full adjacency with R3 on a different serial interface, implying OSPF is enabled and working generally on R2 (points to a localized policy/filter on the R1-facing interface).

messages.content:
What was observed
- OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 on Serial0/1/0 would not form: R1 saw R2 in state INIT, R2 reported no neighbor on that interface — routes between R1 and R2 were not being exchanged.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Inbound packet filtering on R2 Serial0/1/0 was blocking OSPF Hellos (effectively RX loss on that link). Removing the filter resolved the asymmetry.

Changes made
- Detached the inbound ACL from router2 Serial0/1/0.
- Bounced the interface (shutdown / no shutdown).
- Ensured OSPF process 1/router-id 2.2.2.2 is present and set the interface OSPF cost to 49.

Verification — passed
- OSPF adjacency established: router2 Serial0/1/0 shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) in state ospf-nbr-full.
- Interface Serial0/1/0 is admin up / oper up with input_errors/output_errors = 0.
- Running-config shows no inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 (only an outbound ACL "R2_TO_R1").

Remaining work
- None identified. OSPF adjacency and route exchange with R1 restored.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- OSPF adjacency between R1 and R2 on Serial0/1/0 would not form: R1 saw R2 in state INIT, R2 reported no neighbor on that interface — routes between R1 and R2 were not being exchanged.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Inbound packet filtering on R2 Serial0/1/0 was blocking OSPF Hellos (effectively RX loss on that link). Removing the filter resolved the asymmetry.

Changes made
- Detached the inbound ACL from router2 Serial0/1/0.
- Bounced the interface (shutdown / no shutdown).
- Ensured OSPF process 1/router-id 2.2.2.2 is present and set the interface OSPF cost to 49.

Verification — passed
- OSPF adjacency established: router2 Serial0/1/0 shows neighbor 1.1.1.1 (10.0.0.1) in state ospf-nbr-full.
- Interface Serial0/1/0 is admin up / oper up with input_errors/output_errors = 0.
- Running-config shows no inbound ACL on Serial0/1/0 (only an outbound ACL "R2_TO_R1").

Remaining work
- None identified. OSPF adjacency and route exchange with R1 restored.
